title: Annual Operating Budget
description: Prepare a draft operating budget and outgoings view for a commercial property, including assumptions, recoverability logic, and a short variance scenario analysis.
whyItMatters: Budgeting is one of the most visible tests of whether a property manager understands the asset. A thoughtful budget shows control over vendor assumptions, lease recoveries, service quality, and risk. A lazy budget creates disputes, missed savings, and avoidable owner frustration.
skillsLearned: Scenario analysis for NOI and expense movement
Linking building operations to financial planning

steps.title: Build the prior-year baseline
steps.description: Create a clean prior-year actuals table and identify one-offs, cyclical items, and recurring defects that should or should not flow into next year's budget.
steps.tips: If you cannot explain the last 12 months, you are not ready to forecast the next 12.

steps.title: Set assumptions by cost category
steps.description: Draft assumptions for utilities, cleaning, security, repairs and maintenance, management fees, insurance, and contract escalations. State the reason for each assumption.
steps.tips: Treat each line as an operating decision, not just a number.

steps.title: Apply lease and recovery logic
steps.description: Separate recoverable, capped, grossed-up, and owner-borne costs. Document any lease-driven constraints that affect how costs can be recovered.
steps.tips: Budget logic that ignores lease structure only postpones the problem until reconciliation season.

steps.title: Model upside and downside cases
steps.description: Create at least two variance scenarios for major drivers such as electricity, repairs, or security and explain how they would affect NOI and owner guidance.
steps.tips: Owners value managers who show the edges of the plan, not just the center line.

steps.title: Present the draft budget
steps.description: Write a short owner brief summarizing key changes, major risks, proposed contingencies, and any capex items that sit outside the operating budget.
steps.tips: Keep the math detailed but the summary simple.

deliverables: A draft annual operating budget spreadsheet
A recoverability or outgoings treatment note
A scenario analysis for at least two material cost lines
A one-page owner summary explaining the budget

rubric.description: The budget is internally consistent, logically structured, and grounded in realistic assumptions.
Recoverability and owner-borne costs are separated correctly and clearly explained.
Sensitivity cases show genuine understanding of where the budget could move and why.
The owner summary explains the budget without requiring the reader to decode the spreadsheet first.
